title: Rígsþula
stanzas: In ancient days did Rig, the god,
Go wandering through the world of men,
And as he walked the earthly paths,
He came upon a lowly cottage small.
The door was open to his approach,
And he entered in without a sound,
An old man sat beside the fire warm,
And with him sat a woman gray and worn.
Rig was given food and rest,
And a place to sleep beside their hearth,
And when the morning light came forth,
A son was born unto that house.
They called the boy Thræl in those days,
And he grew up to be strong and dark,
His hands were made for labor hard,
And he toiled both day and into night.
From Thræl came forth a race of men,
Who were the thralls and slaves of all,
With rough and dark and weathered skin,
Forever bound to serve the free.
Then Rig continued on his way,
Until he came to a house more fine,
Where a man and woman sat in state,
With comfort round them on every side.
Again Rig was given food and bed,
And in the morning, a son was born,
They called him Karl, the farmer strong,
Who learned to work the fertile ground.
From Karl came forth the race of men,
Who were the farmers, sturdy and true,
With ruddy face and healthy frame,
They worked the soil and raised their kin.
Once more Rig went on his way,
Until he came to a palace grand,
Where a king and queen sat in their state,
With treasures and with gold around.
Again Rig was given rest and food,
And in the morning, a son was born,
They called him Jarl, the noble one,
Who learned to rule with wisdom and might.
From Jarl came forth the race of kings,
The noble ones who ruled the land,
With faces fair and golden hair,
They held dominion over all men.
